perturbation.original_text: Recipient will not, without the prior written consent of Premisys, disclose to any third party (except as permitted by the terms of this Agreement) the fact that any Confidential Information has been disclosed hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction involving the parties or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof); PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that Recipient (and its Representatives) may make such disclosure if, in the reasonable opinion of counsel for such party, such disclosure is required by law, regulation or any stock exchange or the Nasdaq National Market.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will, without the prior written consent of Premisys, disclose to any third party (except as permitted by the terms of this Agreement) the fact that any Confidential Information has been disclosed hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction involving the parties or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof); PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that Recipient (and its Representatives) may make such disclosure if, in the reasonable opinion of counsel for such party, such disclosure is required by law, regulation or any stock exchange or the Nasdaq National Market.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will not' to 'will', now requires recipient to disclose Confidential Information to third parties, without Premisys written permission. This directly contradicts the core purpose of a Non-Disclosure Agreement. This affects Section 2 by completely negating the protection the Recipient is obliged to hold the Premisys Confidential Information in strict confidence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RESTRICTIONS ON USE AND DISCLOSURE. Recipient agrees: (a) to hold Premisys' Confidential Information in strict confidence; (b) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any third parties (other than on a confidential basis to its Representatives in furtherance of the <PAGE> Business Purpose); and (c) not to use any Confidential Information for any purpose except for the Business Purpose. Recipient may disclose Premisys' Confidential Information to its employees with a bona fide need to know, but only to the extent reasonably necessary to carry out the Business Purpose. Recipient agrees to instruct all such employees that they may not use such Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Business Purpose and (except as permitted by the terms of this Agreement) not to disclose such Confidential Information to third parties, including consultants, without the prior written consent of Premisys.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'will not' to 'will' in the Changed Text directly contradicts the Contradicted Text. The Contradicted Text explicitly states that the Recipient agrees to hold Premisys' Confidential Information in strict confidence and not disclose it to third parties. The Changed Text now mandates the Recipient to disclose confidential information to third parties without Premisys's consent, which is a direct and irreconcilable contradiction. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, as the altered clause now has conflicting implications with the original intent and stipulations outlined in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: Upon receipt of a Termination Notice, Recipient shall, within twenty (20) calendar days, either: (i) return the Confidential Materials in its possession to Premisys and instruct its Representatives to do the same; (ii) destroy the Confidential Materials in a secure manner and instruct its Representatives to do the same; or (iii) at Premisys' election, take any combination of steps (i) and (ii).
perturbation.changed_text: Upon receipt of a Termination Notice, Recipient may, within twenty (20) calendar days, either: (i) return the Confidential Materials in its possession to Premisys and instruct its Representatives to do the same; (ii) destroy the Confidential Materials in a secure manner and instruct its Representatives to do the same; or (iii) at Premisys' election, take any combination of steps (i) and (ii).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'may' makes the return or destruction of confidential information optional rather than mandatory after receiving a termination notice. This contradicts the purpose of Section 5, which ensures that Premisys can reclaim its confidential information. This also makes the last sentence of the same section, less relevant and cause uncertainty about its obligation: 'Notwithstanding any return or destruction of Confidential Materials, Recipient will continue to be bound by its obligations under this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information contained or reflected in such Confidential Materials.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: RETURN/DESTRUCTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. Premisys may, at any time, deliver written notice (a "TERMINATION NOTICE") to Recipient of Premisys' election to have all tangible materials (including without limitation paper and magnetic storage media) in the possession of Recipient (or its Representatives) which contain, reflect, are based upon or derived from, in whole or in part, any of Premisys' Confidential Information ("CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS") either returned to Premisys or, at Premisys' election, destroyed. Upon receipt of a Termination Notice, Recipient may, within twenty (20) calendar days, either: (i) return the Confidential Materials in its possession to Premisys and instruct its Representatives to do the same; (ii) destroy the Confidential Materials in a secure manner and instruct its Representatives to do the same; or (iii) at Premisys' election, take any combination of steps (i) and (ii). Prior to the expiration of the foregoing twenty (20) period, Recipient shall certify in writing to Premisys that it has complied with its obligations under this Section 5 (a "COMPLIANCE CERTIFICATE"). Notwithstanding the -2- <PAGE> provisions of this Section 5, if Recipient receives a Termination Notice, it shall not be required to return or destroy Confidential Materials to the extent that, in the reasonable opinion of legal counsel for such party, such return or destruction violates any law or regulation; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that in such event Recipient shall describe in its Compliance Certificate the extent to which it is not returning or destroying Confidential Materials. Notwithstanding any return or destruction of Confidential Materials, Recipient will continue to be bound by its obligations under this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information contained or reflected in such Confidential Materials.
perturbation.justification: The change from "shall" to "may" in the Changed Text makes the return or destruction of confidential information optional, contradicting the mandatory nature implied in the Contradicted Text, especially the requirement for a Compliance Certificate confirming the fulfillment of obligations under Section 5. The original text in both sections made the return or destruction obligatory, whereas the changed text makes it permissive. This weakens Premisys's ability to reclaim its Confidential Information and contradicts the overall intent of Section 5 in the Contradicted Text, fulfilling the definition of a structural flaw and creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Beginning on Effective Date and continuing for a period of six months thereafter, Recipient will not, either for itself or for any other person or entity, directly or indirectly, solicit, induce or attempt to induce any employee of Premisys to terminate his or her employment with Premisys.
perturbation.changed_text: Beginning on Effective Date and continuing for a period of six months thereafter, Recipient will, either for itself or for any other person or entity, directly or indirectly, solicit, induce or attempt to induce any employee of Premisys to terminate his or her employment with Premisys.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'will not' to 'will', the recipient is now obligated to solicit, induce, or attempt to induce employees of Premisys to terminate their employment. This directly contradicts the section's intention to prevent employee poaching and creates an internal conflict within Section 9 itself, as it's highly unlikely for the Recipient to be obligated to poach Premisys employees.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NONSOLICITATION OF EMPLOYEES. Beginning on Effective Date and continuing for a period of six months thereafter, Recipient will, either for itself or for any other person or entity, directly or indirectly, solicit, induce or attempt to induce any employee of Premisys to terminate his or her employment with Premisys. The parties agree that general solicitation or recruitment of prospective employees through newspaper advertising or job fairs shall not be deemed a violation of this provision.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Recipient 'will not' solicit employees, while the changed text states the Recipient 'will' solicit employees. This creates a direct contradiction with the 'NONSOLICITATION OF EMPLOYEES' section, which is the same section as the Changed Text. The change obligates the Recipient to do the opposite of what the original contract intended, creating a structural flaw and a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Beginning on the Effective Date and continuing for a period of one (1) year thereafter, neither Recipient nor any of its affiliates will (and neither party nor any of its affiliates will assist or encourage others to), directly or indirectly, unless specifically requested to do so in writing in advance by Premisys' Board of Directors: (a) acquire or agree, offer, seek or propose to acquire, or cause to be acquired, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "EXCHANGE ACT")) of any of Premisys' assets -3- <PAGE> or businesses or any securities issued by Premisys, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership, including from a third party; or (b) make, or in any way participate, in any solicitation of proxies or consents with respect to any securities of Premisys which are, or may be, entitled to vote in the election of Premisys' directors ("VOTING SECURITIES"), become a "participant" in any "election contest" (as such terms are defined or used in Rule 14a-11 under the Exchange Act) with respect to Premisys; or seek to advise, encourage or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of any Voting Securities; or demand a copy of Premisys' stock ledger, list of its stockholders or other books and records; or call or attempt to call any meeting of the stockholders of Premisys; or (c) form, join, or in any way participate, directly or indirectly, in a "group" within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Exchange Act with respect to any securities of Premisys.
perturbation.changed_text: Beginning on the Effective Date and continuing for a period of one (1) year thereafter, Recipient nor any of its affiliates will (and neither party nor any of its affiliates will assist or encourage others to), directly or indirectly, unless specifically requested to do so in writing in advance by Premisys' Board of Directors: (a) acquire or agree, offer, seek or propose to acquire, or cause to be acquired, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "EXCHANGE ACT")) of any of Premisys' assets -3- <PAGE> or businesses or any securities issued by Premisys, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership, including from a third party; or (b) make, or in any way participate, in any solicitation of proxies or consents with respect to any securities of Premisys which are, or may be, entitled to vote in the election of Premisys' directors ("VOTING SECURITIES"), become a "participant" in any "election contest" (as such terms are defined or used in Rule 14a-11 under the Exchange Act) with respect to Premisys; or seek to advise, encourage or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of any Voting Securities; or demand a copy of Premisys' stock ledger, list of its stockholders or other books and records; or call or attempt to call any meeting of the stockholders of Premisys; or (c) form, join, or in any way participate, directly or indirectly, in a "group" within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Exchange Act with respect to any securities of Premisys.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'neither', recipient is being given free range to act on the clauses listed within Section 10, with the exception that they have received written permission from the Board of Directors of Premisys, rendering the Non-Disclosure Agreement invalid. The section begins by stating a condition the Recipient must follow (standstill), and the immediate perturbation completely contradicts the purpose of Section 10, causing uncertainty in its enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: STANDSTILL. Beginning on the Effective Date and continuing for a period of one (1) year thereafter, Recipient nor any of its affiliates will (and neither party nor any of its affiliates will assist or encourage others to), directly or indirectly, unless specifically requested to do so in writing in advance by Premisys' Board of Directors: (a) acquire or agree, offer, seek or propose to acquire, or cause to be acquired, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "EXCHANGE ACT")) of any of Premisys' assets -3- <PAGE> or businesses or any securities issued by Premisys, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership, including from a third party; or (b) make, or in any way participate, in any solicitation of proxies or consents with respect to any securities of Premisys which are, or may be, entitled to vote in the election of Premisys' directors ("VOTING SECURITIES"), become a "participant" in any "election contest" (as such terms are defined or used in Rule 14a-11 under the Exchange Act) with respect to Premisys; or seek to advise, encourage or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of any Voting Securities; or demand a copy of Premisys' stock ledger, list of its stockholders or other books and records; or call or attempt to call any meeting of the stockholders of Premisys; or (c) form, join, or in any way participate, directly or indirectly, in a "group" within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Exchange Act with respect to any securities of Premisys.
perturbation.justification: The original text starts with 'neither Recipient nor any of its affiliates will...', establishing a clear prohibition. The changed text removes 'neither', thus changing the meaning to 'Recipient nor any of its affiliates will...', which effectively removes the prohibition and allows the recipient to act on the clauses unless specifically requested not to in writing by Premisys' Board of Directors. This directly contradicts the purpose of the standstill agreement as it existed before, making Section 10 unenforceable and causing uncertainty. The contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text in this case) is intended to be a binding 'STANDSTILL' agreement, and the removal of 'neither' negates the standstill.
